title: Missing External Dependency Orchestration
purpose: Identify and resolve the root cause of test execution failures. Initial perception was 'Vitest hangs on ALL execution.' Diagnostic investigation revealed three distinct root causes: (1) npx vitest hangs due to npm update checks, (2) vmThreads pool deadlocks on Node 24 + macOS, (3) Redis-dependent tests timeout when Docker offline.
architectural_constraints: No opportunistic config changes without hypothesis
Each experiment must be isolated and checkpointed
Root cause must be reproducible before fix is applied
Fix must not break existing build:frontend or typecheck

deliverables.description: Documented reproduction matrix: commands, hang timing, CPU behavior, worker behavior
Audit of Vitest, Vite, Next.js, React, Node versions and config interactions
Fast-fail infrastructure preflight checks for integration tests

acceptance_criteria: vitest runs empty test suite and exits cleanly
vitest runs single unit test and exits cleanly
vitest runs full unit suite and exits cleanly
Root cause documented with reproduction steps
Fix verified with 3 consecutive successful runs
build:frontend still passes after any config changes
typecheck still passes after any config changes

traceability.description: Systematic diagnostic investigation identified THREE root causes: (1) npx vitest hangs on npm update checks, (2) vmThreads pool deadlocks on Node 24 + macOS, (3) Redis-dependent tests hang when Docker offline. Applied fixes: direct binary paths, pool: forks, fast-fail preflight checks, conditional test skipping.

failure_modes.mitigation: Run test suite 5x consecutively before declaring fixed
Run full validation gate after every config change

root_causes.actual_cause: npm update checks and package resolution in npx
Vitest 3.2.4 vmThreads deadlock on Node 24 + macOS
Docker offline, Redis unavailable, ioredis reconnect loop
root_causes.fix: Fast-fail preflight checks (500ms), conditional test skipping
